title: Gabriel
summaries: "Gabriel" by George Sand is a dialogued novel published in 1839. Set in Renaissance Italy, it follows a young noble raised as a man to secure an inheritance, only to discover the truth of their biological sex. Confronted with society's hypocrisy, the protagonist rebels against the stark divide between men's privileges and women's oppression. Written in an ambiguous form between novel and theatrical play, this work explores questions of identity and gender inequality in a rigidly hierarchical world.
